civics.description: What started as a simple broadcast of sacred rituals, has since blossomed into a massive media conglomerate. Seers and priests were replaced by charismatic entertainers, all competing to gain a modicum of attention as they appeal to the cosmic storms themselves.
civics.tooltip: - Available Buildings:  - Replaces [entertainer.GetIcon] [entertainer.GetName] Jobs with Storm Dancer Jobs - Species Traits:  - The Amusement Megaplex Corporate Building generates additional Branch Office Value and adds +50 Merchant jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Geo-Engineering Inc.
civics.description: The employees of this Megacorp are used to living inside a golden cage. Accordingly, the corporation will build one wherever they go.
civics.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies: Technologies to remove on the Homeworld - Technologies to remove Natural Blockers are granted as Permanent Research Options upon Colonization - Mastery of Nature replaced with Cultivated Worldscaping Planetary  - For each Natural Blocker on Colonized :  - Resources from Jobs: -10%  - Pop Growth Speed: -10%  - Pop Upkeep: +15% - The Executive Retreat Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.
civics.negative_tooltip: - Homeworld: Starts with two Natural Blockers

civics.name: Antiquarian Expertise
civics.description: There is money to be made in antiques and collectibles, which this society knows better than anyone else. Experts in material speculation, they now look skyward for investment opportunities.
civics.tooltip: - Guaranteed Research Options: ['technology:tech_galactic_archivism', Galactic Archivism] - Initial ['concept_specimen', Specimens]: One §0Common of each Collection Category  - Gain Unity each time you find a Specimen or complete an Archaeological Site  - Purchases from the Curator Enclave: $@galactic_curators_curator_enclave_purchase_cost|%0$ - Enables the Decentralized Research Edict, increasing Research Station Output but decreasing [GetResearcher] Job Output - Replaces Entertainer Jobs with Museum Curator Jobs - The Public Relations Firm Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Space Ranchers
civics.description: Originally an elite guild of animal ranchers, this Megacorporation has developed the technology to raise and care for the voidborne creatures in its home system.
civics.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - ['technology:tech_gravity_wells', Gravity Snares]  - ['technology:tech_alien_cloning', Artificial Breeding]  - Available Starbase Buildings: ['concept_beastport', Beastports]  - Home System: Start with a   - Starting Military Ships are replaced with Space Amoebas  - Starting :  - §0Common Space Amoeba  - The Fast Food Chain Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Sequenced Securities
civics.description: The genetic sequence of every customer and employee is recorded in a database that anyone can access for a modest fee. Enforcement agencies rely on this service intensively to identify nefarious actors from even the smallest residues.
civics.tooltip: - The Private Security Company Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Decentralized R&D
civics.description: Regular inter-department innovation competitions spur this corporation's R&D initiatives.
civics.tooltip: - Factions produce Research instead of Unity   - Factions will form in your empire shortly after the game starts   - The Private Research Enterprises Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Corporate Vampires
civics.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This Megacorporation drains valuable metabolism from idle population via parasitic psychic connections. Once properly collected, these energies can be redistributed to senior management.
civics.tooltip: Enables the following Edicts:  - ['edict:energy_drain',Energy Drain]  - ['edict:sanguine_council',Sanguine Council]  - ['edict:psychic_feast',Convergence of Will] Can construct: . Leaders start with the trait. The Executive Retreat Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Fortune Enterprise
civics.description: This corporation claims to have discovered a ritualistic mechanism to predict the future, leveraging esoteric pattern recognition and cyclical anomaly analysis. To maximize outcomes, future gains and losses are weighed alongside the KPIs of ideal celestial alignments.
civics.tooltip: Replaces:  - Priests with Fortune Tellers  - High Priests with Fortune Brokers Enables ['edict:galactic_dowsing',Galactic Dowsing] Every 13 years, the changes. Fortune Events will periodically benefit or impair your empire.

civics.name: Judicial R&D
civics.description: This Megacorporation has actioned an exciting initiative to reduce judicial inefficiencies while maximizing scientific innovation. Problematic citizens are reassigned to experimental research projects, undergoing rigorous testing to achieve technological breakthroughs. Fatality metrics are maintained within projected expectations.
civics.tooltip: Starts with situation, which grants technology insight upon completion. Can construct and starts with: . Can construct Corporate Building. All non-Machine slaves get access to the Slave Job. Access to : Abominations

civics.name: Influential Cartel
civics.description: This Megacorporation's most powerful offices conduct extraplanetary operations on a strictly need-to-know basis. If you need to ask, you don't need to know.
civics.tooltip: Clandestine will emerge after contacting other empires. Starts with the ability to conduct . [bureaucrat.GetIcon] [bureaucrat.GetNamePlural] will produce Unity per active and successful Proxy War. Starts in with two neighboring empires. Infiltration Level gained on empires after First Contact: +$@secret_society_infiltration_gain|0$ The Corporate Embassy Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Chosen Executives
civics.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Since their founding, this corporation has owed its success to a mysterious Shroud entity, and has been operating under a pact of patronage ever since.
civics.tooltip: - Starts the game in contact with a Shroud Patron. - Additional Starting Technologies:  - The Psionic Offices Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital.

civics.name: Corporate Thinktank
civics.description: This society universally adopted an experimental product in the form of specialized habitation vats. As a result, they are heavily reliant on assistive equipment and incapable of engaging directly in physical work.
civics.tooltip: Adds the trait. Rural Districts have their Worker Jobs fully Automated. ['concept_colonist', Colonist] Jobs are Specialists. Start with insight on ['technology:tech_assembly_pattern', Assembly Patterns] Technology.  Urban Districts have additional Production Overseers jobs. The Virtual Entertainment Studios Corporate Building generates additional Trade and adds +200 Trader jobs to our Capital. origins.name: Prosperous Unification
origins.description: Through its strife and triumphs, this society has reached every young civilization's ambition: a homeworld with unified goals, and a path open to the stars.
origins.tooltip: - Initial Pops: +400 - Homeworld:  - ['concept_generator_district', Generator Districts]: +1  - ['concept_farming_district', Agriculture Districts]: +1  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_planetary_unification', Planetary Unification]

origins.name: Shroud-Forged
origins.description: The guidance of a Shroud entity comes with a cost. Independent automata have started to operate freely among this networked society. Cut off from the Machine Intelligence, the demands of these units are ever-increasing.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - : +4  - Start with 1000  - Start with Situation: Forged by the Shroud - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Unique Building:  - Available Planetary Decision:

origins.name: Mindwardens
origins.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks access to .  This civilization's ancestors were plagued by otherworldly forces that invaded the mind, inspiring them to dedicate great effort and resources into developing anti-psionic technology.  Their ancient devices are still functional, but remain untested against psionic threats from the wider galaxy.
origins.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Home System: Start with a Starhold. - Homeworld:  - Gain access to the Psionic Containment Casus Belli against psionically ascended Empires. - Guaranteed Habitable Worlds do not appear. - Mindwarden Enclaves are not generated at the start of game.

origins.name: Endbringers
origins.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This civilization has been long-obsessed with a mysterious calamity which turned a neighboring planet into a desolate and shrouded world. Now, as they take to the stars, they can finally explore its secrets and find out if a similar fate awaits them too.

origins.name: Evolutionary Predators
origins.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This species has always hunted the most unique creatures, driven by an instinct they didn't fully understand. Possessing a rare ability to integrate the genetics of their prey, it evolved into the apex predator of its world, absorbing every genetic advantage available. Now, their hunger leads them to the stars.
origins.tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Situation:  - Homeworld:

origins.name: Starlit Citadel
origins.description: A mysterious portal looms at the edge of this civilization's home system, a gateway from which hostile entities emerge in relentless waves. These invasions are as unpredictable as they are inevitable, forcing the people to rely on their greatest technological triumph - the Deep Space Citadel.
origins.tooltip: - Home System:   - at the edge of the gravity well   - ['concept_wormhole', Strange Portal] leading to an unknown system  - Homeworld:   -   - ['concept_bioship_remains', Biological Ship Remains]   - ['concept_invasion_site', Invasion Site]: +2  - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_deep_space_citadel_tech', Deep Space Citadel]

origins.name: Wilderness
origins.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This entity is nothing less than a sapient ecosphere: a multitude of diverse lifeforms which long since coalesced to form a gestalt consciousness on a planetary scale.
origins.tooltip: - Species Trait:  - Replaces all standard , , and with bespoke ones - Replaces Colony Ship Colonization with  - Unlocks new Resource:  - Homeworld:  - Size: 18  - ['concept_scorched_plains', Scorched Plains]

origins.name: Treasure Hunters
origins.description: Instilled with a thirst for adventure, this civilization is ready to set forth on their greatest and most challenging expedition. Armed only with a mysterious map, they now race across the stars to secure a legendary pirate treasure.
origins.tooltip: - Starts with an inactive ['r_celestial_chart_0_desc', Celestial Chart] - Every recruited leader has a bonus trait - Ruler Traits:  -   -   -

origins.name: Primal Calling
origins.description: The rustle of bladed grass. The roaring silence of the night. The will of the hunter, sharp and cold as starlight...  This civilization has a primal connection to the wild. Viewing themselves as stewards to the diverse (and often deadly) wildlife native to their world, they now turn their gaze skyward. Masters of animal handling and ranching, this relationship with fauna has developed according to their ethics. How this bond will evolve on a galactic scale remains to be seen.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Home System: Space Fauna in a neighboring system - Available Buildings: ['concept_wildlife_ranch', Wildlife Ranch] - Can choose a special regarding Space Fauna upon First Contact - Additional Starting Technologies: ['technology:tech_genome_mapping', Genome Mapping] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['technology:tech_alien_life_studies', Xenology] - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_gravity_wells', Gravity Snares] after First Contact with Space Fauna - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_alien_cloning', Artificial Breeding] after capturing Space Fauna - Gain insight on ['technology:tech_controlled_mutations', Controlled Mutations] after cloning Space Fauna

origins.name: Storm Chasers
origins.description: There is no challenge they will not face, no lead they'll fail to chase - and no storm they will ignore.  At its core, this is a civilization seeking thrills. To its benefit, and to its detriment.

origins.name: Synthetic Fertility
origins.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Once a thriving society, a novel genetic disease has left this species unable to reproduce. Running out of options and facing extinction, they place their hope in digital salvation.

origins.description: Their planet is barren, but their home system is a bounty of riches. To exploit it, these machines built bigger than ever before. A mighty Arc Furnace now dominates their sky, but these master engineers are far from satisfied.  The galaxy awaits, and the possibilities are endless. All it takes is the ingenuity to seize them.

origins.description: A massive rift in space and time looms near this civilization's homeworld. After studying the phenomenon for millennia, recent advances in starship technology will allow them to finally reach out and discover what secrets it might hold.

origins.name: Broken Shackles
origins.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin  This civilization began as a mutiny aboard a slaver vessel, and is made up of a diverse group of pre-FTL species.
origins.tooltip: - Initial Pops: Species from across the galaxy  - Starting Planet:   - Planetary Modifier   - Planetary Feature   -  - Starting System:  - One ['concept_advanced_empires', Advanced AI Empire] and several Pre-FTL Civilization regardless of the Galaxy Settings  - One Pre-FTL Civilization: Will be this empire's species and homeworld  - One Pre-FTL Civilization: Uplifted with the Payback origin
origins.negative_tooltip: - Starting Planet:   - Is NOT this empire's homeworld   - Planetary Modifier   - ['concept_ship_debris_broken_shackles', Ship Debris]: +5  - Starting System: Resource Deposits are unexploited  - Missing Starting Technologies:   - ['concept_basic_science_lab_1', Scientific Method]  - ['concept_planetary_government', Planetary Government]   - ['concept_corvettes', Corvettes]  - Policies:   - Cannot select Slavery Allowed   - Cannot select Purging Allowed

origins.name: Fear of the Dark
origins.description: A century ago, one of the planets in this empire's home system was destroyed by a freak asteroid. Some believed this was a malicious attack by an alien species. Others brushed off these concerns as mere paranoia. The tension between the two groups grew so great that a newly-terraformed planet was granted to the fearmongers.
origins.tooltip: - Home System: ['concept_preftl_civilizations', Pre-FTL Civilization] created by a Separatist Faction, formerly part of your Empire, granting events and benefits

origins.name: Overtuned
origins.description: This civilization uses any and all means of organic enhancement, no matter the risk, no matter the cost. After all, it is better to burn out brightly than fade away into obscurity.
origins.tooltip: - Species Traits: Access to ['concept_overtuned_traits', Overtuned] Traits - Overtuned Traits:  - Can be boosted by the Edict  - Can be added and removed through Genetic Modification - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_gene_tailoring', Gene Tailoring]

origins.name: Knights of the Toxic God
origins.description: Centuries ago, this species' homeworld was visited by a Toxic Entity. The ecological catastrophe that followed ruined half the world, but allowed an early unification of the survivors in their reverence for the Toxic God. An Order of Knights has been created to search for the Toxic God, and although many things have changed, the quest continues.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:   -   - Unique ['concept_pc_habitat', Orbital Habitat]:   - ['concept_order_keep', Order's Keep]   - ['concept_orders_demesne', Order's Demesne]   - ['concept_politician', Politician] Jobs replaced by  - Situation:  - Policies: Knightly Duties  - Available Holdings: ['concept_knight_commandery', Order's Commanderies] on Subject colonies

origins.name: Progenitor Hive
origins.description: This hive has gained evolutionary advantage through semi-independent leaders. These "Offspring" greatly improve overall proficiency, though they require constant oversight.
origins.tooltip: - ['concept_spawning_pools', Spawning Pools] replaced with ['concept_offspring_nests', Offspring Nest] - Available Holdings: ['concept_offspring_holding', Offspring Nest] - If Devouring Swarm Civic is NOT selected: Sectors can be released as Subjects - Assigned Leaders: Gain additional passive experience - Available Starbase Buildings: Offspring Outlook - Can construct Offspring Ships

origins.name: Subterranean
origins.description: This civilization may never know why their ancestors migrated below the surface. Was it necessary to ensure self-preservation, or did the promise of bountiful resources allure them? Whatever the case, they successfully adapted, leading to a more environmentally versatile community.
origins.tooltip: - Species Traits:  - ['concept_mining_district', Mining Districts]: Uncapped
origins.effects: mining_district_housing_add: +200; planet_orbital_bombardment_damage: -0.75; planet_structures_cost_mult: +10.0%; planet_structures_upkeep_mult: +10.0%; planet_building_build_speed_mult: -10.0%; planet_storm_devastation_mult: -25.0%

origins.name: Subterranean Machines
origins.description: This civilization has flawlessly integrated into subterranean existence, initially driven by either calculated self-preservation or a directive to locate of hidden, abundant resources in the depths. The resulting mechanical adaptations have unearthed a versatile and unwavering community of machines.
origins.tooltip: - Species Traits:  - ['concept_mining_district', Mining Districts]: Uncapped
origins.effects: mining_district_housing_add: +200; planet_orbital_bombardment_damage: -0.75; planet_structures_cost_mult: +10.0%; planet_structures_upkeep_mult: +10.0%; planet_building_build_speed_mult: -10.0%; planet_storm_devastation_mult: -25.0%

origins.name: Slingshot to the Stars
origins.description: Once, the skies above this civilization were illuminated by shooting stars; then came a long season of darkness. Taking to the heavens in search of answers, the first offworld explorers discovered a dormant (and long abandoned) Quantum Catapult Single Array.
origins.tooltip: - Home System: Nearby ruined with the  - Effects: Increased to +50% Ship Fire Rate

origins.name: Teachers of the Shroud
origins.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Long before their first steps onto the galactic stage, this civilization has been supported and shaped by a Shroudwalker coven.
origins.tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Available Starbase Buildings:  - Communications with the  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_psionic_theory', Psionic Theory].

origins.name: Imperial Fiefdom
origins.description: Our first endeavor beyond our home planet was a painful one, suddenly and brutally subjected to the whims of a foreign galactic power. Now we look towards a future in which we no longer live in the shadow of another empire.
origins.tooltip: - Subject of an  - Can select a ['concept_specialist_subject', Specialist Subject] type at the start of the game

origins.name: Here Be Dragons
origins.description: Since time immemorial, this civilization has shared its star system with a formidable dragon. The mysterious creature's behavior sways between distant benevolence and haughty indifference. How this relationship will evolve as the planet dwellers take to the stars remains to be seen.
origins.tooltip: - Home System: Inhabited by a protective Sky Dragon, that will become wrathful if displeased

origins.name: Ocean Paradise
origins.description: Since its birth, this civilization has enjoyed peace in a generous environment with no natural enemies, enabling unhindered growth and opulence.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (size: 30)  - Planetary Modifiers:   - Planetary Features:  - : Replaced with ['concept_pc_frozen', Frozen Worlds]

origins.name: Subaquatic Machines
origins.description: Since its creation, this machine civilization has delved deep into the underwater realms of an abundant ocean untouched by natural adversaries.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (size: 30)  - Planetary Modifiers:   - Planetary Features:  - : Replaced with ['concept_pc_frozen', Frozen Worlds]
origins.negative_tooltip: - Requires Machine Template System Technology for Species Modification

origins.name: Necrophage
origins.description: This civilization evolved as a parasite, forced to feed off other sapient life forms to sustain itself. As it arrives on the galactic stage, other species will either be brought into the fold, or become its food. For the necrophage, there can be no other way.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - Prepatent Pops: 1.2K  - : Replaced with
origins.negative_tooltip: - Only Necrophage Pops can be Rulers or recruited as Leaders

origins.name: Remnants
origins.description: This civilization once spanned the void, controlling much of the galaxy. They were eventually defeated and almost destroyed, but after a long period of destitution they are returning to the stars.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - (Size: 22)  - ['concept_ruined_arcology', Ruined Arcology]: +6 [GetRemnantAncientRelicEffect]

origins.name: Shattered Ring
origins.description: This civilization inhabits a Ring World built by unknown forerunners. If it can be understood and fully repaired it will grant them great power.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - : Replaced with Colonizable Ring World Segments that have Blockers decreasing Habitability  - Species Traits:

origins.name: Void Dwellers
origins.description: This civilization has made its home in space for as long as it has maintained records - life in a typical planetary environment is anathema to them. They live in several orbital habitats that were only recently united.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - ['concept_void_dweller', Void Dweller]  - ['concept_habitat_preference', Habitat Preference] - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_habitat_1', Orbital Habitats] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_habitat_2', Habitat Expansion] - : Resource-rich systems instead of Colonizable Planets  - Can construct advanced housing buildings on Habitats - Waives Technology requirements for Basic District Specialization on Habitats

origins.name: Voidforged
origins.description: At first, the creators of this machine civilization designed them for eternal space travel, fully optimizing them to function seamlessly in zero gravity environments. After enduring eons of isolation, these machines have recently restored unity among the orbital habitats scattered throughout their home system.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:  - ['concept_void_dweller_2', Zero-G Optimized]  - ['concept_habitat_preference', Habitat Preference] - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_habitat_1', Orbital Habitats] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_habitat_2', Habitat Expansion] - : Resource-rich systems instead of Colonizable Planets  - Can construct advanced housing buildings on Habitats - Waives Technology requirements for Basic District Specialization on Habitats
origins.negative_tooltip: - Requires Machine Template System Technology for Species Modification

origins.name: Scion
origins.description: A Fallen Empire has meddled in the development of this civilization for thousands of years, guiding them onto a path of their choosing.
origins.tooltip: - ['concept_scion', Scion] of a Overlord - Home System: Connected to Overlord's capital via a  - Fallen Empire present in the galaxy regardless of Galaxy settings - Chance of receiving powerful gifts from Overlord

origins.name: On the Shoulders of Giants
origins.description: This civilization has hidden boons in their solar system, placed there in a distant past by a mysterious benefactor.
origins.tooltip: - Home System: related to a mysterious benefactor
origins.negative_tooltip: - Precursor Event Chains blocked until this mystery is solved

origins.name: Common Ground
origins.description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Finding strength in their differences, they soon decided to face the future, and whatever it might bring, together.
origins.tooltip: - Leader of a non-Hegemony  - : Replaced with Federation members - Additional Starting Technologies:

origins.name: Hegemon
origins.description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Gradually, over the span of a century, they masterminded the birth of an interstellar union in which they would have a dominant role.
origins.tooltip: - Leader of a  - : Replaced with Federation members - Additional Starting Technologies:

origins.name: Doomsday
origins.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin This civilization's homeworld is highly unstable, and it is only a matter of time before it explodes. Their only hope is to seek refuge elsewhere before it is too late.
origins.negative_tooltip: - Homeworld: Planetary Modifier  - : Are NOT spawned

origins.name: Calamitous Birth
origins.description: Not native to their 'homeworld', these Lithoids arrived when a meteorite slammed into the planet and killed off most of the native life.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  -   - : +4 - Available Colony Ships: Lithoid Meteorites - Planets Colonized with Lithoid Meteorites:  - Buried Lithoids: +2  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Available Holdings: ['concept_experimental_crater', Experimental Crater] on (Subject) colonies

origins.name: Life-Seeded
origins.description: This civilization's home is a paradise, possibly designed just for them.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld: (Size: 30) with Rare Planetary Features - Species Traits:

origins.name: Post-Apocalyptic
origins.description: Baptized by nuclear fire, this civilization has faced total annihilation - and survived. Devastated yet unbroken, they have rebuilt civilization from the ashes of their old world.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:

origins.name: Radioactive Rovers
origins.description: In the aftermath of a devastating nuclear disaster, these machines emerged as the sole survivors of a once-flourishing civilization. By continuing to fulfill their designated purpose, they gradually rebuilt society from the uninhabitable ashes of their old world.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Species Traits:

origins.name: Clone Army
origins.description: A species of short-lived and infertile soldier clones exceptionally proficient in matters of war, left masterless and alone to question their engineered purpose and uncertain fate.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld: : +2  - Species Traits:  - Additional Starting Technologies:   - ['concept_cloning', Cloning]   - ['concept_gene_banks', Gene Banks]

origins.name: Resource Consolidation
origins.description: This Machine Intelligence has long-since consolidated all resources in their home system into their capital world, covering it entirely with machinery.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  -   - Planetary Features:   - If combined with the Driven Assimilator Civic:  - Planetary Features:  - Waives Technology requirements for the Machine Worlds Ascension Perk
origins.negative_tooltip: - Home System: Planets are ['concept_pc_molten', Molten Worlds] or ['concept_pc_barren', Barren Worlds] devoid of any resources

origins.name: Hard Reset
origins.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin  Abruptly severed from the Machine that assimilated them, this nascent civilization has fought fiercely to establish its own presence in the galaxy.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Home System: Contains a with an related to a decimated ['concept_preftl_civilizations', Pre-FTL Civilization] - Species Traits:  - Limited access to Traits  -   -

origins.name: Mechanist
origins.description: This civilization has been preoccupied with the idea of metallic automatons since the early Steam Age. Although many said it could not be done, the first true robots left the assembly lines long before even rudimentary space flight was achieved.
origins.tooltip: - ['concept_robots', Robot] Pops: 800  - Mechanical Trait:  - Homeworld: ['concept_robot_assembly_plant', Robot Assembly Plant] - Additional Starting Technologies:  - ['concept_powered_exoskeletons', Powered Exoskeletons]  - ['concept_robotic_workers', Artificial Workforce] - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_robomodding', Machine Template System]

origins.name: Syncretic Evolution
origins.description: A second species forms an integral part of this civilization. They are big, strong, and most of them have the intelligence of a particularly dim-witted child. Ancient wars have culled their species of their most aggressive tendencies, leaving them quite servile.
origins.tooltip: - Organic Pops with Trait: 1.2K

origins.name: Tree of Life
origins.description: This Hive evolved in a symbiotic relationship with a vast Tree. The Tree grants them many benefits, but its loss would cripple them.
origins.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - Colonized Planets: Planetary Feature - Colonies without the Tree of Life Sapling: Planetary Decision - Available Holdings: ['concept_tree_of_life_holding', Tree of Life Sprout] on Subject colonies
origins.negative_tooltip: - Colonies without the Tree of Life Sapling: Planetary Modifier

origins.name: Fruitful Partnership
origins.description: This verdant civilization has developed a unique synergy with spaceborne fauna. By offering their fruits to these creatures, they entice the seed dispersers to aid their colonization efforts.
origins.tooltip: - Space Fauna: Disperse ['concept_seed_pod', Seed Pods] throughout the galaxy  - Available Starbase Buildings: ['concept_sm_feeder', Starseed Gardens]  - Available Bombardment Stances: ['concept_seed_bombing' ] - Home System: ['concept_tiyanki' ] in a neighboring system

origins.name: Galactic Doorstep
origins.description: This civilization has a dormant Gateway in their home system - potential technological benefit, or a looming menace?
origins.tooltip: - Home System: Dormant

origins.name: Lost Colony
origins.description: This civilization originated as a lost and forgotten colony, separated from its homeworld long ago. The struggling colonists endured many hardships before they could build up the necessary technological and industrial base that would allow for a return to space.
origins.tooltip: - Starting Planet: Planetary Modifier - An of the same species is present in the galaxy
origins.negative_tooltip: - Starting planet is NOT this empire's homeworld.

all.name: Corvée System
all.description: This society considers it the absolute right of the state to decide where its citizens live and work.
all.tooltip: - Waives all Unity costs for Pop Resettlement.

all.name: Imperial Cult
all.description: This society has a dominant state religion where the ruler is worshiped as a living deity.
all.tooltip: - Ruler's Home Planet has additional [GetPriestIcon] [GetPriest] Jobs

all.name: Beacon of Liberty
all.description: This society is a shining beacon of light in a sea of darkness. Liberty and individual freedoms are held in the highest regard here.

all.name: Exalted Priesthood
all.description: To guard against heresy, this society is ruled by a religious council consisting of the wisest and most pious members of the clergy.
all.tooltip: Capital Buildings replace Politician Jobs with High Priests

all.name: Philosopher King
all.description: It is not enough to simply rule. The Ship of State must be guided by a king that wields enough wisdom and knowledge to steer it true.

all.name: Meritocracy
all.description: An individual's social station or personal connections should have no bearing on their profession. The sole basis for advancement in this society is demonstrated ability and talent.

all.name: Citizen Service
all.description: Are you doing your part? Full citizenship and the political responsibility that comes with it is limited to those who have served a tour of duty in the military. Service guarantees citizenship.
all.tooltip: Grants access to the Recruitment Office Holding

all.name: Technocracy
all.description: To maximize efficiency, this society is governed according to the principles of science and rationality. The personal whims of an ignorant and dangerously unqualified political elite must not be allowed to interfere.
all.tooltip: - Capital Buildings replace Politician Jobs with Science Directors - Scientists start with random Expertise trait - Expertise traits have additional effects - Starts with an additional Scientist

all.name: Feudal Society
all.description: This society is organized in a feudal manner, with a monarch whose rule relies on powerful vassals that govern their territories with considerable autonomy.
all.tooltip: - Ignore Diplomatic Requirements when proposing Subjugation - Your Subjects cannot have the following Agreement Terms:  Expansion Prohibited  Do not join subject wars  Limited Diplomacy

all.name: Police State
all.description: To quash any traces of dissent, the population in this repressive society is carefully monitored and controlled by a large internal police force.

all.name: Idealistic Foundation
all.description: This society was founded on strong idealistic values. Whether the current government remains true to them or not, the people have not forgotten.

all.name: Environmentalist
all.description: This society seeks to co-exist in harmony with nature. Great care is taken to preserve the environment and limit consumerism where possible.
all.tooltip: - Available Buildings:   - Additional Starting Buildings: Ranger Lodge  - Available Holdings:

all.name: Slaver Guilds
all.description: Much of the true political power in this society rests with a number of powerful and ruthless slaver guilds. They know how to get the most out of a slave.

all.name: Inward Perfection
all.description: This calm and pacifist society has little use for strangers who do not understand its way of life. More than anything, these people would prefer to be left alone.
all.tooltip: Cannot have Rivals Cannot form Defensive Pacts Cannot form Migration Treaties Cannot form Research Agreements Cannot form Commercial Pacts Cannot Guarantee Independence Cannot join Federations Cannot Infiltrate pre-FTLs Cannot have forced Subjects Cannot use Unrestricted Wars policy
all.negative_tooltip: Blocks the Minister of State Council Position
all.effects: country_unity_produces_mult: +20.0%; founder_species_growth_mult: +10.0%; country_edict_fund_add: +50; pop_citizen_happiness: +0.05; envoys_add: -1; intel_encryption_add: +1; intel_decryption_add: -1

all.name: Warrior Culture
all.description: This society has developed into a hardy warrior culture. Martial prowess is valued above all else, and true glory can only be found on the field of battle.
all.tooltip: - Replaces Entertainer Jobs with Duelist Jobs. - Duelists turn Alloys into Unity, Amenities, and Naval Capacity. - Replaces Conserved Fauna Jobs with Gladiatorial Beast Jobs.

all.name: Distinguished Admiralty
all.description: The Fleet and the Admiralty have unusually prominent roles in this society, wielding a great deal of influence in political circles. They have the pick of the litter when it comes to new military recruits.

all.name: Free Haven
all.description: This society has a well-earned reputation as a free haven. The tired, the poor, the huddled masses yearning to breathe free - all are welcome here, regardless of their species or origin.

all.name: Cutthroat Politics
all.description: The political system in this society is renowned for its intrigue. Power struggles, shady backroom deals and cloak and dagger scheming are par for the course. Those who survive long enough to learn the game, however, tend to learn it well.

all.name: Corporate Dominion
all.description: This society is dominated by a megacorporation that has completely supplanted the role of the state.
all.tooltip: Starts with the Offworld Trade Companies technology already unlocked Unlocks Ship Type: Private Colony Ship

all.name: Agrarian Idyll
all.description: A simple and peaceful life can often be the most rewarding. This agrarian society has, to a large extent, managed to avoid large-scale urbanization.
all.tooltip: Agriculture Districts provide 400 more Housing. Generator Districts and Mining Districts provide 300 more Housing. City Districts provide 200 less Housing.  Farmers also produce 2 Amenities.

all.name: Shadow Council
all.description: Unbeknownst to its own citizens, this society is actually manipulated from behind the scenes by a secretive shadow council. Appearances must be kept, but the tyranny of the majority should also be guarded against. After all, what if the fools vote for the wrong candidate?

all.name: Mining Guilds
all.description: Several large mining guilds have reached a dominant position in this society. The government relies heavily on their support.

all.name: Parliamentary System
all.description: The parliamentary system in this society encourages a free and lively debate. Currying favor with one of the dominant political factions can prove to be quite advantageous.
all.tooltip: Factions will form in your empire shortly after the game starts.

all.name: Efficient Bureaucracy
all.description: This society is renowned for its efficiency. Not only do the mag-trains run on time, but the colossal bureaucratic apparatus required to run an interstellar nation has been greatly streamlined.
all.tooltip: Bureaucrats and [GetPriestIcon] [GetPriestPlural] provide Edict Fund scaling with their Unity production.

all.name: Nationalistic Zeal
all.description: A strong sense of nationalistic pride permeates all layers of this society.

all.name: Functional Architecture
all.description: This society is renowned for its simple yet functional architecture. There are those who would refer to this building style as boring or even depressing, but in most cases, neo-concrete does the job just as well as any other building material.
all.tooltip: - Waives Technology requirements for Basic

all.name: Aristocratic Elite
all.description: This society has an entrenched nobility that occupies the upper echelons of society.
all.tooltip: - Capital Buildings replace Politician Jobs with Nobles - Can construct Noble Estates buildings that add additional Noble Jobs - Grants access to the Noble Chateaus Holding - Leaders have a 20% chance to start with an additional positive and negative trait.

all.name: Shared Burdens
all.description: This society believes in an equitable distribution of resources, making little to no distinction between the needs of ruler and ruled. All work together for the benefit of the whole.
all.tooltip: Allows Shared Burdens Living Standard, under which all Pops have moderate Consumer Goods Upkeep regardless of Strata Disables the use of most other Living Standards Grants access to the Communal Housing Outreach Holding

all.name: Fanatic Purifiers
all.description: This society appears hellbent on scouring the galaxy of all other sapient life. Come what may, they will suffer no xenos to live.
all.tooltip: Cannot engage in Diplomacy with other species! Cannot access the Galactic Market Has the 'Purification' casus belli on other species Xeno Pops will always be purged Gains Unity from purging Xeno Pops May use Armageddon bombardment stance
all.negative_tooltip: Blocks the Minister of State Council Position

all.name: Barbaric Despoilers
all.description: This society holds few things sacred. To fight is to live, and the strongest may seize whatever they covet.
all.tooltip: Cannot form Migration Treaties Cannot form (but can join) Federations other than Martial Alliances or Hegemonies Has the 'Despoliation' casus belli on all other empires Unlocks the Raiding bombardment stance, which in turn allows us to abduct pops from besieged planets and resettle them to our own worlds. Decreased Opinion for most other empires

all.name: Byzantine Bureaucracy
all.description: This society is largely governed by a complex and, to the outsider, almost labyrinthine system of bureaucracy. An army of officials and functionaries work tirelessly to keep the government running smoothly and ensure no citizens are allocated resources they cannot demonstrate a properly filed and triple-stamped need for.

all.name: Merchant Guilds
all.description: A number of powerful and very influential merchant guilds have risen to prominent positions in this society. They hold significant sway with the government.
all.tooltip: Capital Buildings provide Merchant Jobs

all.name: Reanimators
all.description: Within this society, death is no bar to the call to arms. Masters of the art of necromancy reanimate corpses to raise dread hosts and guardians, striking fear into the hearts of lesser mortals.
all.tooltip: - Soldiers are replaced with Necromancers - Available Buildings:  - Available Holdings:  - Defeated Organic Armies are sometimes resurrected as Undead Armies - Defeated organic Leviathans can sometimes be resurrected - Controlled cloned organic Space Fauna can be reanimated

all.name: Diplomatic Corps
all.description: This society has a long tradition of rhetoric and debate, and celebrate those who are able to get their way using only words.

all.name: Death Cult
all.description: This society's religion is built around periodic, ritual sacrifice. Willing initiates devote themselves to a decade of study before meeting an end whose effects ripple throughout their culture.
all.tooltip: - Available Buildings:   - Additional Starting Buildings: Sacrificial Temple   - Available Holdings:   - Access to   - Priests are converted into Death Priests

all.name: Memorialists
all.description: This empire pays tribute to memories and sapients of the past, deriving greater stability and insight from the continuous cycles of death, rebirth and legacy.
all.tooltip: - Can build Sanctuary of Repose - Sanctuaries of Repose improve Governing Ethics Attraction on Tomb Worlds and Relic Worlds - Death Chroniclers turn Consumer Goods into Unity and Stability, and inherit the Ethics-based modifiers of [civilian.GetIcon] [civilian.GetNamePlural]

all.name: Catalytic Processing
all.description: By focusing their industrial efforts on catalytic chemistry, this civilization has unlocked the key to using organic materials in the construction of spacebound structures.
all.tooltip: - [foundry.GetIcon] [foundry.GetNamePlural] are replaced with Catalytic Technicians  - [foundry.GetIcon] [artisan.GetIcon] Industrial Jobs have their Minerals Upkeep replaced with Food Upkeep.

all.name: Masterful Crafters
all.description: A penchant for meticulous crafting lies at the heart of this society. Deft appendages and keen sensory organs aid them in creating truly wondrous treasures even in the most basic of trades.
all.tooltip: Artisans are replaced with Artificers  Artificers turn Minerals into Consumer Goods and Trade

all.name: Pleasure Seekers
all.description: This society rejoices in its own perfection. Those who are elevated by their privileged social structure have surrendered to a culture which glorifies pleasure and entertainment, regardless of a cost typically borne by others.
all.tooltip: - [civilian.GetIcon] [civilian.GetNamePlural] are converted into Hedonists, which do not produce Amenities   - Allows Decadent Lifestyle Living Standard, under which all Pops have increased Happiness, Consumer Goods Upkeep and Amenities Usage   - Hedonists with Decadent Lifestyle provide Unity and Pop Growth (or Assembly) Speed, but require Amenities   - Additional Starting Buildings:

all.name: Idyllic Bloom
all.description: This society has long dedicated itself to environmental care and improvement, striving to create a true paradise for its inhabitants.
all.tooltip: Can build Gaia Seeders on your and your vassal's worlds ideal to your starting species. Facilities spread across the planet progressively bring it closer to becoming a Gaia World, through 4 upgrade phases. As you unlock improved Terraforming technologies, Gaia Seeders can be constructed on additional planet types.

all.name: Anglers
all.description: This society is well-adapted to an aquatic environment, using the bounty of the sea to fuel its economy.
all.tooltip: - On Ocean Worlds, Agriculture Districts are uncapped.  - On Wet worlds, Agriculture Districts have the Aquaculture Specialization.
all.negative_tooltip: - Requires the trait.

all.name: Marine Machines
all.description: This mechanical society is engineered for seamless operation in diverse aquatic environments and excels in crafting unparalleled marine infrastructure to drive its economy.
all.tooltip: - On Ocean Worlds, Agriculture Districts are uncapped.  - On Wet worlds, Agriculture Districts have the Aquaculture Specialization.
all.negative_tooltip: - Requires the trait.

all.name: Pompous Purists
all.description: A society so utterly convinced of their own superiority, that any attempt at diplomacy not initiated by themselves will be utterly ignored.
all.tooltip: Can only engage in Diplomacy with other empires if they are the proposer.

all.name: Mutagenic Spas
all.description: This civilization engages in ritualistic mutagenic bathing, with professional attendants overseeing the use of highly dangerous substances. This 'invigorating' process may appall uninitiated onlookers.
all.tooltip: - Can build Mutagenic Spas. - Mutagenic Spa Attendants turn Consumer Goods into Amenities, Pop Growth and Monthly Organic Pop Growth Multiplier but reduce Happiness and Habitability.   Urban District Development (and Industrial Specialization) increase some of these effects.

all.name: Relentless Industrialists
all.description: To live is to exploit. This civilization is built around the principle of living life to the fullest, in the sense of squeezing the maximum profit from every last ounce of matter and membrane available for exploitation.
all.tooltip: Can build Coordinated Fulfillment Center, significantly increasing Alloy and Consumer Goods output at the cost of reduced Pop Growth and gradually turning planets into Tomb Worlds.

all.name: Scavengers
all.description: In this society, nothing goes to waste, even in the grimmest times of war: no enemy technology will go undiscovered, as teams of scrappers accompanying the war fleets scrape wreckages of anything and everything worth salvaging.
all.tooltip: - Researching Debris grants both Research and a portion of other Resource Costs of the destroyed fleet - Ships can be salvaged from some Debris

all.name: Ascensionists
all.description: The ultimate goal of this empire is to ascend to ever greater levels of fulfillment.
all.tooltip: - Colonies have +1 Ascension Tier on game start.

all.name: Vaults of Knowledge
all.description: The knowledge, principles, and experiences of exceptional people are digitized and accumulated in the Vaults of Knowledge. Citizens can access this source of wisdom and experience to learn from the ghosts of the past.
all.tooltip: Can build unique Vaults of Knowledge Vaults of Knowledge produce Unity and increase Leader Experience Gain based on Destiny Traits acquired.

all.name: Crusader Spirit
all.description: The lack of guidance creates weak minds - which easily succumb to corruption and plunge into decadence. This society has assumed the self-imposed mantle of bringing "enlightenment" to the masses through aggressive proselytizing.
all.tooltip: Commanders start with the Crusader Trait Can only use Liberation Wars policy

all.name: Eager Explorers
all.description: Yearning for the stars, this civilization sped through several stages of development, reaching the age of galactic exploration far earlier than most.
all.tooltip: Start with 2 Exploration Vessels and an Engineering Vessel equipped with Subspace Drives, making them incapable of Hyperlane travel but granting limited Jump Drive abilities. Gain an increased chance to discover FTL Travel Technologies
all.negative_tooltip: - Start at a technological and infrastructural disadvantage, and with 1K fewer Pops than normal

all.name: Hyperspace Specialty
all.description: This society has always embraced speed. The manipulation of hyperspace is just another thoroughfare, and those with aptitude in the field have risen to the highest caste.
all.tooltip: Hyperlane Breach Points is Guaranteed Research Option. Has Low Intel on all systems within 3 Hyperlanes of your starting system.

all.name: Dimensional Worship
all.description: This society believes that a divine existence lies beyond the folds in space and time. Systems where instabilities are highest hold a sacred meaning to them as sites of both pilgrimage and research.
all.tooltip: - Available Starbase Buildings: ['starbase_building:dimensional_shrine', Dimensional Shrine]

all.name: Heroic Past
all.description: The legendary heroes of the past inspire each new generation to achieve the extraordinary. As role models, government officials embody these ideals.

all.name: Oppressive Autocracy
all.description: Only the elite of the elite deserve the best of the best. Everyone else is expected to provide for the ruling class and their lackeys.
all.tooltip: Force Dystopian Society Living Standard, under which non-Elite Jobs have decreased Consumer Goods Upkeep Only Elite Jobs and [enforcer.GetIcon] [enforcer.GetNamePlural] have Amenities Upkeep Most Pops generate 1 additional Crime Only Elite Jobs can produce Amenities Forbids Holo-Theatres

all.name: Selective Kinship
all.description: In an uncertain universe, only kin can be trusted. This civilization is selective about the species it defines as equals.

all.name: Dark Consortium
all.description: A deep understanding of the architecture of the universe allowed this society to exploit the mysterious properties of dark matter long before the discovery of the hyperdrive.
all.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - ['technology:tech_mine_dark_matter', Dark Matter Drawing]   - Access to   - Has access to the Dark Matter Breakthrough Council Agenda.

all.name: Natural Design
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths WARNING: Unable to modify organic species  This society is adamant that nature has already created perfection in its people's genetic code. They have long devoted their medical and governmental services to the health and preservation of natural, untouched genome sequences.
all.tooltip: Available Buildings:
all.negative_tooltip: - Lowers approval of empires that have started Ascension Paths

all.name: Sovereign Guardianship
all.description: This collection of warring tribes has developed a historical preference for entrenched territories and defensive posturing. Now united, they are prepared to bring this philosophy to the stars, aiming to keep their borders solid and tensions low.
all.tooltip: Unlocks Fanatic Guardians as defense armies. Allows building a Reloading Bay in starbases. Enables Inner Focus Edict, converting Influence into Unity.
all.effects: empire_size_colonies_mult: +1; empire_size_branch_office_mult: +1; empire_size_systems_mult: +1.5; empire_size_pops_mult: -50.0%; empire_size_districts_mult: -50.0%; diplo_weight_mult: +20.0%

all.name: Genesis Guides
all.description: This society is devoted to the creation of emerging species. As the stewards of evolution's potential, they allocate portions of their worlds to safeguard the development of pre-sapient life forms.
all.tooltip: - Available Colony Ships: Genesis Ark - Planets Colonized with Genesis Arks:  - Pre-Sapient Pops: +300   - Planetary Features: Genesis Preserve  - Gains Unity from uplifting Pre-Sapient Species
all.negative_tooltip: - Cannot build Colony Ships - Cannot build Alien Zoos

all.name: Lubrication Tanks
all.description: This machine empire improved upon its basic drone-shell production through the vigorous application of highly aggressive lubrication agents, controlled by carefully programmed terminals. This 'optimized' process relies on typically wasted byproducts from the industries that are now repurposed for maximal efficiency.
all.tooltip: - Can build Hyper Lubrication Basins. - Lubrication Terminals turn Alloys into Pop Assembly but increase Amenities Usage.

all.name: Rapid Replicator
all.description: This society has taken control of the means of self-assembly. Each machine begets another, forming an unbroken chain stretching our planet's limits.

all.name: Static Research Analysis
all.description: This society excels in predictive analysis within research methodologies, ensuring the pursuit of only the most promising leads.

all.name: Warbots
all.description: This society constantly refines its armies, economy, and education for diverse warfare strategies. Each machine contributes to collective strength, ensuring adaptability and success in conflict.

all.name: Astrometeorology
all.description: With deep respect for the forces of nature, this species goes to great lengths to uncover the inner workings of environmental phenomena. As they set out across the stars, they take with them a particular interest in astrometeorology. Central to their ambitions are the most fantastic and potentially destructive of all astronomical events, the cosmic storms themselves.
all.tooltip: - Available Buildings:   - Additional Starting Buildings: Astrometeorology Observatory

all.name: Storm Devotion
all.description: Ever since ancient times, this society has been able to attract tempests through their rituals. Over the years, they combined technical innovations to enhance their efficiency. With the dawn of the space age, they now seek to take this knowledge to the stars.
all.tooltip: - Available Buildings:  - Replaces [entertainer.GetIcon] [entertainer.GetName] Jobs with Storm Dancer Jobs - Species Traits:

all.name: Planetscapers
all.description: After eons of carefully cultivating a perfect balance on their homeworld, this society is ready to tend to even the most inhospitable environments among the stars. At their core, these people believe nature can be shaped, controlled, and perfected. Not to tame, but in service of perfect beauty.
all.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies: Technologies to remove on the Homeworld - Technologies to remove Natural Blockers are granted as Permanent Research Options upon Colonization - Mastery of Nature replaced with Cultivated Worldscaping Planetary  - For each Natural Blocker on Colonized :  - Resources from Jobs: -10%  - Pop Growth Speed: -10%  - Pop Upkeep: +15%
all.negative_tooltip: - Homeworld: Starts with two Natural Blockers

all.name: Galactic Curators
all.description: The meticulous collection and preservation of historical artifacts has always fascinated this civilization. But now, with the mysteries of the galaxy at their doorstep, there is everything left to learn.
all.tooltip: - Guaranteed Research Options: ['technology:tech_galactic_archivism', Galactic Archivism] - Initial ['concept_specimen', Specimens]: One §0Common of each Collection Category  - Gain Unity each time you find a Specimen or complete an Archaeological Site  - Purchases from the Curator Enclave: $@galactic_curators_curator_enclave_purchase_cost|%0$ - Enables the Decentralized Research Edict, increasing Research Station Output but decreasing [GetResearcher] Job Output - Replaces Entertainer Jobs with Museum Curator Jobs

all.name: Beastmasters
all.description: Coming to full, technological maturity in a system that serves as a feeding ground for Space Amoebas, this society has acquired a deep knowledge of their life cycle. They now work to nurture, raise, and ultimately clone these creatures for mutual benefit.
all.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - ['technology:tech_gravity_wells', Gravity Snares]  - ['technology:tech_alien_cloning', Artificial Breeding]  - Available Starbase Buildings: ['concept_beastport', Beastports]  - Home System: Start with a   - Starting Military Ships are replaced with Space Amoebas  - Starting :  - §0Common Space Amoeba

all.name: Genetic Identification
all.description: This society requires every citizen to be registered from birth in a genetic index used in every level of bureaucracy.

all.name: Crowdsourcing
all.description: This society has realized that individual inspiration is the root of true innovation. Open-source patents, open-mindedness, spontaneous collaborations and innovation bounties pave the way to progress.
all.tooltip: - Factions produce Research instead of Unity   - Factions will form in your empire shortly after the game starts

all.name: Civil Education
all.description: In this empire, education has supplanted law enforcement. Through comprehensive preparation and strict course correction, they ensure that no individual is beyond the reach of learning. After all, there are no bad citizens - only bad educators.
all.tooltip: - Replaces all with   - Replaces with

all.name: Entropy Drinkers
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This society drains valuable metabolism from idle population via parasitic psychic connections. Once properly collected, these energies can be redistributed to the more important classes.
all.tooltip: Enables the following Edicts:  - ['edict:energy_drain',Energy Drain]  - ['edict:sanguine_council',Sanguine Council]  - ['edict:psychic_feast',Convergence of Will] Can construct: . Leaders start with the trait.

all.name: Superstitious Beliefs
all.description: This society claims to have discovered a ritualistic mechanism to predict the future, leveraging esoteric pattern recognition and cyclical anomaly analysis. Proper ceremonies must be performed before every major decision to harmonize with the unseen forces that shape their destiny.
all.tooltip: Replaces:  - Priests with Spirit Mediums  - High Priests with High Astrologists Enables ['edict:galactic_dowsing',Galactic Dowsing] Every 13 years, the changes. Fortune Events will periodically benefit or impair your empire.

all.name: Experimental Sentencing
all.description: Judicial processing and thirst for knowledge share a single solution in this society. Working in utmost secrecy, researchers conduct harsh experiments on test subjects drawn from the criminal underworld. The occasional fatality is the cost of progress.
all.tooltip: Starts with situation, which grants technology insight upon completion. Can construct and starts with: . All non-Machine slaves get access to the Slave Job. Access to : Abominations

all.name: Secret Societies
all.description: A cabal of elite and secretive figures weave complicated political threads behind the scenes of this society, entwining internal affairs with those of other worlds.
all.tooltip: Clandestine will emerge after contacting other empires. Starts with the ability to conduct . [bureaucrat.GetIcon] [bureaucrat.GetNamePlural] will produce Unity per active and successful Proxy War. Starts in with two neighboring empires. Infiltration Level gained on empires after First Contact: +$@secret_society_infiltration_gain|0$

all.name: Chosen
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Influenced by the Shroud in their early history, this society is bound to a pact of patronage with a powerful entity.
all.tooltip: - Starts the game in contact with a Shroud Patron. - Additional Starting Technologies:

all.name: Tankbound
all.description: This society's members live out their existences suspended in vats of specialized bio-fluids. As such, they are heavily reliant on assistive equipment and incapable of engaging directly in physical work.
all.tooltip: Adds the trait. Rural Districts have their Worker Jobs fully Automated. ['concept_colonist', Colonist] Jobs are Specialists. all.name: Prosperous Unification
all.description: Through its strife and triumphs, this society has reached every young civilization's ambition: a homeworld with unified goals, and a path open to the stars.
all.tooltip: - Initial Pops: +400 - Homeworld:  - ['concept_generator_district', Generator Districts]: +1  - ['concept_farming_district', Agriculture Districts]: +1  - Planetary Modifiers:  - Guaranteed Research Options: ['concept_planetary_unification', Planetary Unification]

all.name: Shroud-Forged
all.description: The guidance of a Shroud entity comes with a cost. Independent automata have started to operate freely among this networked society. Cut off from the Machine Intelligence, the demands of these units are ever-increasing.
all.tooltip: - Homeworld:  - : +4  - Start with 1000  - Start with Situation: Forged by the Shroud - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Unique Building:  - Available Planetary Decision:

all.name: Mindwardens
all.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks access to .  This civilization's ancestors were plagued by otherworldly forces that invaded the mind, inspiring them to dedicate great effort and resources into developing anti-psionic technology.  Their ancient devices are still functional, but remain untested against psionic threats from the wider galaxy.
all.tooltip: - Additional Starting Technologies:  - Home System: Start with a Starhold. - Homeworld:  - Gain access to the Psionic Containment Casus Belli against psionically ascended Empires. - Guaranteed Habitable Worlds do not appear. - Mindwarden Enclaves are not generated at the start of game.

all.name: Endbringers
all.description: WARNING: Challenging Origin WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This civilization has been long-obsessed with a mysterious calamity which turned a neighboring planet into a desolate and shrouded world. Now, as they take to the stars, they can finally explore its secrets and find out if a similar fate awaits them too.

all.name: Evolutionary Predators
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This species has always hunted the most unique creatures, driven by an instinct they didn't fully understand. Possessing a rare ability to integrate the genetics of their prey, it evolved into the apex predator of its world, absorbing every genetic advantage available. Now, their hunger leads them to the stars.
all.tooltip: - Species Traits:  - Situation:  - Homeworld:

all.name: Starlit Citadel
all.description: A mysterious portal looms at the edge of this civilization's home system, a gateway from which hostile entities emerge in relentless waves. These invasions are as unpredictable as they are inevitable, forcing the people to rely on their greatest technological triumph - the Deep Space Citadel.
all.tooltip: - Home System:   - at the edge of the gravity well   - ['concept_wormhole', Strange Portal] leading to an unknown system  - Homeworld:   -   - ['concept_bioship_remains', Biological Ship Remains]   - ['concept_invasion_site', Invasion Site]: +2  - Additional Starting Technologies: ['concept_deep_space_citadel_tech', Deep Space Citadel]

all.name: Wilderness
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  This entity is nothing less than a sapient ecosphere: a multitude of diverse lifeforms which long since coalesced to form a gestalt consciousness on a planetary scale.

all.name: Treasure Hunters
all.description: Instilled with a thirst for adventure, this civilization is ready to set forth on their greatest and most challenging expedition. Armed only with a mysterious map, they now race across the stars to secure a legendary pirate treasure.
all.tooltip: - Starts with an inactive ['r_celestial_chart_0_desc', Celestial Chart] - Every recruited leader has a bonus trait - Ruler Traits:  -   -   -

all.name: Primal Calling
all.description: The rustle of bladed grass. The roaring silence of the night. The will of the hunter, sharp and cold as starlight...  This civilization has a primal connection to the wild. Viewing themselves as stewards to the diverse (and often deadly) wildlife native to their world, they now turn their gaze skyward. Masters of animal handling and ranching, this relationship with fauna has developed according to their ethics. How this bond will evolve on a galactic scale remains to be seen.

all.name: Synthetic Fertility
all.description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Once a thriving society, a novel genetic disease has left this species unable to reproduce. Running out of options and facing extinction, they place their hope in digital salvation.

all.description: Their planet is barren, but their home system is a bounty of riches. To exploit it, these machines built bigger than ever before. A mighty Arc Furnace now dominates their sky, but these master engineers are far from satisfied.  The galaxy awaits, and the possibilities are endless. All it takes is the ingenuity to seize them.
